commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=71c9d2f1db00aa4e82f0036ac5c90f61049a88af)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Signal plugin handling for timestamp-based native/container quote metadata, quote fallback receipt metadata, focused tests, and Signal docs.

PR surface: Source +135, Tests +157, Docs +1. Total +293 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows core requires `replyTo` when `replyToId` exists, Signal does not declare it, and the new quote fallback catches unclassified Signal RPC failures. I did not run a live Signal roundtrip in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - The two blockers are narrow, mechanical repairs in the Signal adapter/send path and can be attempted without deciding the product direction of native Signal quotes.

target.title: Add native Signal reply quotes
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Signal replies from OpenClaw were sent as ordinary messages even though inbound Signal events already carry stable message timestamps. In busy DMs or groups, that made assistant replies harder to associate with the triggering message.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change threads the inbound Signal timestamp into the existing reply target context and keeps the implementation inside the Signal plugin boundary. When a reply has the matching inbound timestamp and author, native mode sends `quote-timestamp`, `quote-author`, and `quote-message`; container mode translates those to `quote_timestamp`, `quote_author`, and `quote_message` for `/v2/send`. If Signal rejects quote metadata, OpenClaw retries the same reply as an ordinary Signal send so the final answer is not dropped.

## User Impact

Signal DMs and groups can now show OpenClaw responses as native Signal replies when the configured backend supports quote metadata. Existing sends without valid reply metadata keep the previous ordinary-message behavior, and the Signal docs now describe the reply fallback behavior.

## Evidence

Behavioral proof:

- Inbound Signal context now sets `ReplyToId` from the inbound message timestamp so existing reply threading can attach it to outbound payloads.
- Native Signal sends include quote metadata only when the reply timestamp and author are available.
- bbernhard container sends receive the equivalent snake_case quote fields through `/v2/send`.
- Quote-send failures retry as ordinary sends and record `nativeReplyStatus: "fallback"` in receipt metadata.

Automated checks:

- `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts` passed.
- `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/client-container.test.ts extensions/signal/src/client-adapter.test.ts` passed.
- `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts` passed.
- `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts extensions/signal/src/client-container.test.ts extensions/signal/src/client-adapter.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts` passed.
- `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` passed.
- `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 corepack pnpm docs:list` passed.
- `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 corepack pnpm format:docs:check` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.

Screenshots are not included because this is a backend channel transport change with no OpenClaw-rendered UI surface.
target.author: jesse-merhi
